title: NBA Daily Digest
subtitle: Your daily dose of NBA news and updates - July 13, 2026

items.headline: LeBron James Free Agency Saga Continues: Cavs, Heat, Warriors, Sixers Await Decision
items.summary: The NBA world remains on edge as four-time MVP LeBron James, an unrestricted free agent, weighs his options for his 24th season. The Cleveland Cavaliers, Miami Heat, Golden State Warriors, and Philadelphia 76ers are reportedly holding off on further transactions, eagerly awaiting James' decision. Warriors forward Draymond Green has openly pitched to James, while his agent Rich Paul has been relaying voice messages from interested teams.

items.headline: Hornets Trade LaMelo Ball to Timberwolves in Blockbuster Four-Team Deal
items.summary: In a significant off-season move, the Charlotte Hornets have traded star guard LaMelo Ball and Josh Green to the Minnesota Timberwolves. The multi-team deal, which also involved Brooklyn and Chicago, saw Charlotte acquire Naz Reid, a 2033 unprotected first-round pick, multiple future pick swaps (2028, 2029, 2030), second-round picks (2029, 2032, 2033), and a record $40.7 million trade exception. Hornets GM Jeff Peterson stated the move was for long-term organizational success.

items.headline: Pelicans Edge Suns in Close Contest
items.summary: The Pelicans secured a hard-fought victory over the Suns.

items.headline: Pistons Defeat Cavaliers
items.summary: Detroit bested Cleveland in a competitive Summer League matchup.

items.headline: Celtics Pull Away from Hornets
items.summary: The Celtics improved to 2-0 in Summer League, overcoming the Hornets.

items.headline: Warriors Dominate Thunder
items.summary: Golden State put on a strong performance against Oklahoma City.

items.headline: Magic Win Overtime Thriller vs. Blazers
items.summary: Orlando narrowly defeated Portland in an overtime battle.

items.headline: Wizards Cruise Past Kings
items.summary: The Wizards secured a decisive victory over the Kings.

items.headline: Spurs Take Down Bucks
items.summary: San Antonio defeated Milwaukee in their Summer League contest.

items.headline: Clippers Beat Jazz
items.summary: The Clippers emerged victorious against the Jazz.

items.headline: Trevor Keels Explodes for Heat
items.statContext: Miami's Trevor Keels had a game-high 32 points against Orlando in a losing effort.

items.headline: Will Riley Dominates for Wizards
items.statContext: Washington's Will Riley led his team with 32 points in their win over Sacramento.

items.headline: Malik Dia's Efficient Scoring for Pelicans
items.statContext: Undrafted free agent Malik Dia contributed 18 points and 8 rebounds off the bench for New Orleans.

items.headline: Dillon Mitchell Shines for Celtics
items.statContext: Boston's 2026 second-round pick, Dillon Mitchell, showcased an all-around performance against Charlotte.

items.headline: Lanier Leads Pistons with Scoring Outburst
items.statContext: Detroit's Lanier had a strong offensive outing in their victory over Cleveland.

items.headline: Deni Avdija's Breakthrough Season Highlights Trade Talks
items.statContext: Portland's Deni Avdija is a hot topic in trade discussions, with his team-friendly contract and recent 'breakthrough season' stats making him a valuable asset.

items.headline: Tyrese Haliburton Nearing Return After Achilles Tear
items.summary: Indiana Pacers star Tyrese Haliburton is reportedly on track to return for the 2026-27 season opener. Haliburton missed the entirety of last season after suffering a torn right Achilles tendon in Game 7 of the 2025 NBA Finals. Optimism is high within the Pacers organization regarding his recovery.

items.headline: Sandro Mamukelashvili Joins Lakers in Post-LeBron Era Overhaul
items.summary: The Los Angeles Lakers have added Sandro Mamukelashvili in free agency as part of their significant roster changes following LeBron James' departure. Mamukelashvili expressed excitement about joining the prestigious organization and contributing to a 'new page' for the team.

items.headline: NBA Europe Expansion Gaining Traction for 2027 Launch
items.summary: The NBA's plans for a pan-European expansion league, NBA Europe, are progressing, with a projected launch by October 2027. Reports indicate that bids for team franchises are reaching up to $1 billion.

items.headline: Celtics Trade Jaylen Brown to 76ers Amid Luxury Tax Concerns
items.summary: The Boston Celtics have traded five-time All-Star Jaylen Brown to the Philadelphia 76ers. Celtics President Brad Stevens cited the complexities of building a contender under the second apron with significant cap space dedicated to two players as a key reason for the move.
